userStories.title: Schema CashSession + server actions
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a CashSession model that links an operator to a cash register for a work shift, so that each sale can be tied to a specific session.
acceptanceCriteria: CashSessionStatus enum: open | closed
CashSession model: id, cashRegisterId (FK), operatorId, operatorName, status, openedAt, closedAt?, openingBalance, closingBalance?, createdAt, updatedAt
CashRegister model gets cashSessions CashSession[] relation
src/lib/actions/cash-sessions.ts exports openCashSession, closeCashSession, getOpenSessions, getOperatorActiveSession
openCashSession validates cashRegister exists and operator has no open session already
closeCashSession validates session is currently open
getOperatorActiveSession returns the open session for a given operatorId or null
getOpenSessions returns all open sessions with cashRegister name
Each mutation calls revalidatePath('/caixas') and revalidatePath('/pdv')
Typecheck passes
Lint passes
userStories.notes: Depends on existing CashRegister model. operatorName is stored as a snapshot (not a join) so session history is preserved even if user is renamed. openCashSession receives operatorId and operatorName from the caller (server action reads from auth session).

userStories.title: Schema Sale + SaleItem + SalePayment + server action persistFinishedSale
userStories.description: As a developer, I want Sale, SaleItem, and SalePayment models in Prisma and a server action to persist a finished sale from the PDV engine to the database.
acceptanceCriteria: SaleStatus enum: open | finished | cancelled
PaymentMethod enum: cash | pix | debit | credit | store_credit
Sale model: id, sequence (autoincrement), code (VEN-XXXXXX computed), cashSessionId (FK), operatorId, operatorName, status, subtotal, discount, total, notes?, finishedAt?, cancelledAt?, createdAt
SaleItem model: id, saleId (FK), productId (FK), quantity Float, unitPrice Float, total Float
SalePayment model: id, saleId (FK), method PaymentMethod, amount Float
Product model gets saleItems SaleItem[] relation
CashSession model gets sales Sale[] relation
src/lib/actions/sales.ts exports persistFinishedSale
persistFinishedSale receives finished Sale object from sale-engine, creates Sale + SaleItems + SalePayments + StockEntry (type saida) per item in a single Prisma transaction
Typecheck passes
Lint passes
userStories.notes: Depends on US-007. Use db.$transaction for atomicity — if StockEntry creation fails, the Sale is not saved. SaleItem.quantity is Float to support fractional quantities (weight-based products). code field is computed as 'VEN-' + sequence.toString().padStart(6, '0').

userStories.title: PDV redesign — novo layout visual
userStories.description: As an operator, I want the PDV to have a cleaner, denser layout so that I can find products faster and see the total at a glance.
acceptanceCriteria: Customer select removed from header entirely
Header shows: MARKETOPS PDV logo, session info placeholder (caixa + operador), current time, link to Vendas
Product section: compact list rows instead of large cards — each row shows name, SKU, price, stock badge
Low stock product shows amber left border + badge 'Estoque baixo'
Zero stock product is visually dimmed and cannot be added
Total panel: total value shown at the TOP in large dark box, then subtotal/discount/paid/balance below
Finalizar venda button is h-14 and always anchored to bottom of payment panel
Discount input uses Percent icon not Search icon
Visual feedback when product is added: brief highlight on cart row
Existing sale-engine logic (addSaleItem, finishSale, etc.) unchanged
Typecheck passes
Lint passes
userStories.notes: Depends on nothing — purely visual. Do not change sale-engine.ts. Do not change sale-data.ts. Only change pos-content.tsx layout and styles. Products still come from props (will be connected to DB in US-011).

userStories.title: PDV gate de sessao — selecao ao entrar
userStories.description: As an operator, I want to select which cash register I am operating when I open the PDV, so that my sales are linked to the correct session.
acceptanceCriteria: On /pdv load, server component checks if logged-in operator has an active CashSession
If no active session: full-screen session gate modal shown with list of available (closed) CashRegisters to open
Operator selects a register + sets opening balance + confirms to open session
If active session exists: PDV loads normally with session info in header (register name + operator name)
Session info in PDV header replaces the old cashSession select
Opening balance input in session gate defaults to 0
Typecheck passes
Lint passes
userStories.notes: Depends on US-007. pdv/page.tsx becomes async server component. Gate logic runs server-side. CashSession opening happens via server action called from a client gate component.

userStories.title: Conectar PDV ao banco — produtos, sessao e persistir venda
userStories.description: As an operator, I want the PDV to show real products from the database and persist completed sales, so that inventory is updated and sales are recorded.
acceptanceCriteria: PDV loads products from db.product.findMany (active only) instead of initialProducts mock
Remove all imports of initialProducts, initialCashSessions, initialCashRegisters, initialCustomers from pos-content.tsx
PosContent receives products: Product[] and cashSession: ActiveSession from server component as props
handleFinishSale calls persistFinishedSale server action after sale-engine finishSale returns ok:true
On successful persist: router.refresh() reloads products with updated stock
finishSale still validates stock client-side before calling server action
Error from persistFinishedSale shown in errors list
Typecheck passes
Lint passes
userStories.notes: Depends on US-008 and US-010. Keep sale-engine.ts pure — do not add DB calls inside it. Server action is called AFTER sale-engine validation succeeds. Products are refreshed after sale to reflect updated stock from StockEntry records.

userStories.title: HelpRequest schema + server actions
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a HelpRequest model that links a cash register to a help call, so that operators can request supervisor assistance from the PDV.
acceptanceCriteria: HelpRequestStatus enum: active | attended | expired
HelpRequest model: id, cashRegisterId (FK), operatorId, operatorName, cashRegisterName, status (default active), expiresAt (createdAt + 60s), attendedBy?, attendedAt?, createdAt
CashRegister model gets helpRequests HelpRequest[] relation
src/lib/actions/help-requests.ts exports createHelpRequest, attendHelpRequest, getActiveHelpRequests
createHelpRequest validates no active request already exists for the same cashRegisterId
createHelpRequest sets expiresAt = new Date(Date.now() + 60_000)
attendHelpRequest validates status === 'active', updates to attended with attendedBy + attendedAt = now
getActiveHelpRequests calls expireOldRequests first (updates active+expired to expired), then returns all still active ordered by createdAt asc
Each mutation calls revalidatePath('/supervisor') and revalidatePath('/pdv')
Typecheck passes
Lint passes
userStories.notes: Depends on existing CashRegister model. operatorName and cashRegisterName stored as snapshots. getActiveHelpRequests auto-expires stale requests before returning results.

userStories.title: PDV — Botao Chamar Supervisor com contagem regressiva
userStories.description: As an operator, I want a 'Chamar Supervisor' button in the PDV header so that I can request assistance and see a countdown until my call expires.
acceptanceCriteria: Header PDV: Bell icon button 'Chamar Supervisor' in top-right area next to current time
On click: calls createHelpRequest server action with cashSession.cashRegisterId, cashSession.operatorId, cashSession.operatorName, cashSession.cashRegisterName
After successful call: button shows amber 'Aguardando... (60s)' with countdown ticking every second via setInterval
When countdown reaches 0: button resets to default 'Chamar Supervisor' state
While countdown active: button is disabled (no second call possible)
If createHelpRequest returns error (e.g. already active): show error in existing errors list
cashSession prop provides all needed identifiers
Typecheck passes
Lint passes
userStories.notes: Depends on US-012. Only changes pos-content.tsx. Countdown uses local state (number) + setInterval. Clear interval on unmount or when countdown reaches 0. Purely client-side countdown — no polling.

userStories.title: Tela /supervisor — polling 4s, alarme sonoro, badge na aba
userStories.description: As a supervisor, I want a real-time screen showing active help requests with an audible alarm and browser tab badge, so I can respond quickly.
acceptanceCriteria: Route /supervisor: async server component renders SupervisorContent with initialRequests from getActiveHelpRequests()
SupervisorContent is a client component with setInterval 4s polling getActiveHelpRequests server action
Each active request shown as a card: cashRegisterName, operatorName, time remaining (progress bar 0-60s), 'Atender' button
Progress bar color: amber when > 20s remaining, red when <= 20s
Atender button calls attendHelpRequest(id, supervisorName) — supervisorName from useSession().data?.user?.name
Empty state when no active requests: 'Nenhum chamado ativo' centered in slate-800 card
Alarm: Web Audio API oscillator at 440Hz, 200ms burst, repeated every 5s while requests.length > 0; stops when empty
Tab title: document.title set to '(N) Supervisor — MARKETOPS' when N > 0, 'Supervisor — MARKETOPS' when empty
Page header shows 'Supervisor' title + 'Fila de chamados' subtitle
Sidebar link /supervisor already exists for supervisor role (middleware already configured)
Typecheck passes
Lint passes
userStories.notes: Depends on US-012 and US-013. Web Audio AudioContext must be created after first user interaction (browser policy). Create context lazily on first alarm trigger. Polling clears on component unmount. HelpRequestRow type: { id, cashRegisterName, operatorName, createdAt: Date, expiresAt: Date }.
